data.title: Model T Car History
data.previewSummary: The Model T was the first affordable car, produced from 1908 to 1927, revolutionizing personal transportation.
previewBullets: Affordable price for the average consumer
Impact on American society and economy
data.details:
The Model T was designed for simplicity and ease of use. It had a 2.9L engine, 20 horsepower, and a top speed of 40 mph. The car's affordability and durability made it a huge success, with over 15 million units sold.
It was produced in various colors, including black, red, and gray.
The Model T's impact on society was significant, as it made car ownership accessible to the average American.
data.analogy: The Model T is like the first smartphone, making a complex technology accessible to the masses.
examples: The impact on the American economy
misconceptions: The Model T was the first car ever made
The Model T was only available in black
The Model T was a luxury car
formalStructure: Inverse relationship between price and demand

data.title: Internal Combustion Engine Basics
data.previewSummary: Internal combustion engines convert chemical energy into mechanical energy through combustion of fuel-air mixture.
previewBullets: Pistons move up and down to generate power
Crankshaft converts up-and-down motion to rotary motion
data.details:
Air-fuel mixture ignited in cylinders
Pistons move up and down to generate power
Crankshaft converts up-and-down motion to rotary motion
Engine block contains cylinders and crankshaft
Valves control airflow and fuel injection
Fuel injection system provides precise fuel delivery
data.analogy: Engine is like a heart, pumping energy through the vehicle
data.problemPatterns: Engine efficiency and power limited by various constraints

data.title: Henry Ford's Innovations
data.previewSummary: Henry Ford revolutionized manufacturing with the assembly line and the Model T car.
previewBullets: Model T car made cars affordable
data.details:
Henry Ford introduced the assembly line in 1913, reducing production time for the Model T car from 12 hours to 2.5 hours. He also implemented vertical integration, controlling all aspects of production from raw materials to finished cars.
This led to significant cost reductions and made cars affordable for the average consumer.
data.analogy: Henry Ford's assembly line is like an assembly line in a factory, where each worker specializes in a single task to produce a finished product.
mechanism: Standardization of parts leads to efficient production
Division of labor increases worker productivity
Vertical integration reduces costs and increases efficiency
examples: Ford's assembly line production of the Model T car
Toyota's production of the Corolla car
misconceptions: Henry Ford was the first to introduce the assembly line
The assembly line was only used in the automotive industry
data.problemPatterns: Henry Ford's innovations addressed the problem of high production costs and inefficiencies in manufacturing.
formalStructure: Linear equation: y = mx + b (assembly line production)
Exponential curve: y = ab^x (cost reduction through vertical integration)
